articles.headline: Government Launches 'National Digital Public Infrastructure 2.0' for Enhanced Citizen Services
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister unveils an ambitious roadmap for integrating digital services across all government departments, building on the success of UPI and Aadhaar.
• The initiative focuses on interoperability, data security, and citizen-centric design, aiming to bridge the digital divide.
• Key pillars include a federated data architecture, AI-driven grievance redressal, and expansion of digital literacy programs to rural areas.
• Emphasizes a 'whole-of-government' approach to ensure seamless delivery of welfare schemes and public utilities.
• Aims to position India as a global leader in digital public goods, promoting open-source technologies and data sovereignty.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor announced a decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% during the latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting.
• This decision comes as retail inflation shows signs of moderation, though global uncertainties persist.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is adopting a cautious 'wait and watch' approach, monitoring food price volatility and global crude oil prices.
• Forecasts for India's GDP growth rate remain robust, prompting the RBI to prioritize price stability.

prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.

articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Free and Open Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• External Affairs Minister and his Japanese counterpart concluded a productive strategic dialogue in Tokyo, focusing on regional security and economic cooperation.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law and peaceful resolution of disputes.
• Discussions included enhancing defense cooperation, maritime security, and collaboration on critical and emerging technologies.
• India highlighted its Act East Policy, aligning with Japan's vision for a stable and prosperous region.
• Agreements were reached to bolster supply chain resilience and deepen cooperation in infrastructure development across Southeast Asia.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Gen Earth Observation Satellite for Disaster Management
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-07) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
• EOS-07 is equipped with high-resolution optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) payloads, designed for enhanced monitoring of natural disasters.
• The satellite will provide critical data for urban planning, agricultural assessment, and climate change studies, with improved temporal and spatial resolution.
• This mission marks a significant step towards India's self-reliance in space technology and its commitment to global disaster risk reduction efforts.
• The data from EOS-07 is expected to bolster India's capabilities in providing early warnings for floods, cyclones, and droughts.

articles.trivia: ISRO holds the record for launching 104 satellites in a single mission in February 2017, a world record at the time.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Fiscal Federalism Dispute
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment upholding the constitutional rights of states in matters of fiscal federalism, resolving a long-standing dispute over revenue sharing.
• The ruling clarified the scope of states' legislative powers regarding certain taxation matters and the distribution of centrally collected revenues.
• The judgment emphasizes cooperative federalism and the need for greater consultation between the Union and State governments on financial policies.
• Experts believe this decision will have significant implications for future fiscal relations and the autonomy of state governments.
• The Court also called for a review mechanism to address imbalances in financial transfers and grants-in-aid.

articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building, symbolizing judicial authority and constitutional interpretation.
articles.trivia: Article 280 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a Finance Commission to define the financial relations between the central government and the state governments.

articles.headline: National Health Mission Expands Focus to Non-Communicable Diseases in Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare announced a significant expansion of the National Health Mission (NHM), specifically targeting the rising burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in rural India.
• The revamped strategy includes enhanced screening facilities at Health and Wellness Centres (HWCs), early detection campaigns, and improved access to treatment for NCDs like diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular diseases.
• Special emphasis will be placed on training community health workers (ASHAs and ANMs) to conduct basic NCD screenings and promote healthy lifestyles.
• The initiative seeks to integrate NCD management with existing primary healthcare services, reducing the need for rural populations to travel to urban centers for diagnosis and care.
• Increased budgetary allocation and public-private partnerships are envisioned to ensure the success and sustainability of the program.

articles.trivia: NCDs account for over 60% of all deaths in India, making them a significant public health challenge.

articles.headline: India Set to Exceed 2030 Renewable Energy Targets, Eyes Further Ambitions
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced that India is well on track to surpass its 2030 target of 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity.
• Rapid deployment of solar and wind energy projects, coupled with advancements in battery storage technology, is driving this accelerated progress.
• The government is now exploring even more ambitious targets for renewable energy, potentially aiming for net-zero emissions earlier than initially planned.
• Green hydrogen initiatives and offshore wind projects are identified as key growth areas for the next decade.
• This achievement underscores India's commitment to climate action and energy security, attracting significant foreign investment in the sector.

articles.headline: India-Africa Youth Summit Concludes with Focus on Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
articles.explainer:
• The inaugural India-Africa Youth Summit concluded in New Delhi, bringing together young leaders, entrepreneurs, and policymakers from both regions.
• The summit emphasized strengthening bilateral ties through collaboration in skill development, digital education, and fostering youth entrepreneurship.
• Key outcomes included the establishment of an 'India-Africa Youth Exchange Program' and a joint fund to support start-ups led by young Africans and Indians.
• Discussions also covered youth participation in climate action, sustainable agriculture, and cultural exchange.
• This initiative aims to harness the demographic dividend of both continents and build a future-oriented partnership.

articles.headline: Government Unveils Draft 'AI Ethics and Governance Framework' for Public Consultation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a comprehensive draft framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible development and deployment of AI technologies, focusing on principles of fairness, accountability, transparency, and data privacy.
• It proposes guidelines for industries, researchers, and government agencies on ethical AI practices, including bias mitigation and human oversight.
• Public consultation is invited to gather feedback from stakeholders, including tech companies, civil society organizations, and academic institutions.
• This move positions India as a proactive player in shaping global AI governance standards, balancing innovation with societal well-being.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches 'Virasat Darshan' Scheme to Boost Heritage Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism introduced the 'Virasat Darshan' scheme, a new initiative aimed at promoting India's rich cultural and historical heritage through sustainable tourism.
• The scheme focuses on developing integrated tourism circuits around lesser-known heritage sites, including ancient temples, archaeological ruins, and colonial-era structures.
• It emphasizes community participation, skill development for local guides, and digital promotion to attract both domestic and international tourists.
• Infrastructure development, including last-mile connectivity and visitor amenities, will be a key component of the projects under the scheme.
• The initiative seeks to generate local employment, preserve cultural assets, and enhance the overall tourist experience.

articles.headline: National Cyber Security Coordinator Urges Robust Framework for Critical Infrastructure Protection
articles.explainer:
• The National Cyber Security Coordinator emphasized the urgent need for a robust and dynamic cybersecurity framework to protect India's critical information infrastructure (CII).
• Addressing a conference on cyber resilience, he highlighted the escalating threat of state-sponsored cyber-attacks and ransomware campaigns targeting essential services.
• The call includes enhanced collaboration between government agencies, private sector entities, and international partners to share threat intelligence and best practices.
• Focus areas identified are capacity building, development of indigenous cybersecurity solutions, and stringent audit mechanisms for CII.
• The government plans to introduce new regulations to mandate stricter cybersecurity standards for sectors like energy, finance, and telecommunications.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Scheme to Promote Millet Cultivation and Consumption
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a new national scheme aimed at significantly boosting the cultivation, processing, and consumption of millets.
• The initiative, 'Poshan Anaj Abhiyan', offers incentives to farmers for adopting millet farming, provides support for establishing processing units, and promotes millet-based products.
• It aligns with the International Year of Millets (2023) objectives and India's commitment to food and nutritional security.
• Millets are recognized for their climate resilience, nutritional value, and low water footprint, making them a sustainable crop choice.
• The scheme also includes awareness campaigns to educate consumers about the health benefits of incorporating millets into their diet.

articles.headline: India Plans Modernization of Antarctic Research Stations for Enhanced Scientific Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Earth Sciences announced plans for a significant upgrade and modernization of India's permanent research stations in Antarctica, Maitri and Bharati.
• The modernization aims to enhance scientific capabilities, improve logistical support, and ensure the safety and comfort of researchers in extreme Antarctic conditions.
• New state-of-the-art laboratories, advanced communication systems, and sustainable energy solutions are part of the upgrade.
• This initiative underscores India's commitment to Antarctic research, climate change studies, and adherence to the Antarctic Treaty System.
• The upgraded facilities will support long-term studies in glaciology, oceanography, atmospheric sciences, and polar biology.

articles.headline: National Urban Flood Resilience Plan Launched to Mitigate City Flooding
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveiled the 'National Urban Flood Resilience Plan', a comprehensive strategy to combat increasing urban flooding incidents.
• The plan integrates advanced meteorological forecasting, improved drainage infrastructure, and nature-based solutions like urban wetlands and permeable pavements.
• Focus areas include vulnerability mapping, early warning systems, and capacity building for urban local bodies in disaster preparedness and response.
• Pilot projects will be implemented in ten major flood-prone cities, with a phased rollout across other urban centers.
• The initiative emphasizes inter-agency coordination and citizen participation in developing resilient urban ecosystems.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Constructive Dialogue on Indus Water Treaty Implementation
articles.explainer:
• Technical experts from India and Pakistan held a constructive round of talks in Islamabad regarding the implementation of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
• The discussions focused on specific technical issues related to the design and operation of hydroelectric projects on the Western Rivers, as per the treaty provisions.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the IWT, a long-standing framework for water sharing between the two nations.
• While no major breakthroughs were announced, the dialogue was seen as a positive step towards resolving differences through bilateral mechanisms.
• The Permanent Indus Commission is expected to meet again in the coming months to continue these technical deliberations.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Initiates Comprehensive Impact Assessment of Gender Budgeting in India
articles.explainer:

• The study aims to identify best practices, gaps in implementation, and suggest policy refinements to ensure equitable resource allocation for women's empowerment.
• Data will be collected on the expenditure and impact of schemes designed for women's welfare, education, health, and economic participation.
• This assessment is crucial for strengthening the gender budgeting framework and promoting greater accountability in government spending.
• The findings are expected to inform the upcoming Union Budget and state budgets, advocating for more targeted and impactful gender-sensitive policies.

• The program provides financial assistance for purchasing drones for crop monitoring, IoT-enabled sensors for soil health, and precision irrigation systems.
• It aims to enhance agricultural productivity, reduce input costs, and improve climate resilience for vulnerable farming communities.
• Training and technical support will be provided through Krishi Vigyan Kendras (KVKs) and agricultural universities.
• This initiative is part of a broader push to modernize Indian agriculture and leverage technology for sustainable food production.

• It highlights the role of ancient Indian ports, shipbuilding technologies, and cultural exchanges facilitated by maritime trade routes.
• The initiative aims to raise public awareness about India's historical global linkages and its influence on world trade and culture.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tribal Affairs Initiates Review of Forest Rights Act Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs announced a comprehensive review of the implementation of the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006, across various states.
• The review aims to assess the progress in recognizing and vesting forest rights, identify bottlenecks, and ensure the effective empowerment of tribal and forest-dwelling communities.
• Focus areas include the issuance of individual and community forest rights titles, resolution of disputes, and the role of Gram Sabhas in the decision-making process.
• Concerns regarding delays in processing claims, rejection rates, and lack of awareness among beneficiaries will be specifically addressed.
• The findings will inform policy amendments and strengthen the institutional mechanisms for better implementation of the FRA.

articles.headline: Researchers Discover New Species in Western Ghats, Highlighting Biodiversity Hotspot Status
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian researchers announced the discovery of several new species of amphibians and invertebrates in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats.
• The findings underscore the critical importance of the Western Ghats as a global biodiversity hotspot and the need for intensified conservation efforts.
• The newly identified species include a unique frog variant and several previously unrecorded insect species, contributing to the region's ecological diversity.
• Scientists emphasize the threats posed by habitat loss, climate change, and pollution to the fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats.
• The discoveries call for stronger protective measures and sustainable development practices to safeguard this natural treasure.

articles.headline: SEBI Issues Guidelines for Municipal Corporations to Raise Funds through Green Bonds
articles.explainer:
• The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) released detailed guidelines enabling municipal corporations to issue 'Green Bonds' to finance environmentally sustainable projects.
• This move aims to channel private capital towards urban green infrastructure, renewable energy projects, and sustainable waste management initiatives at the local level.
• The guidelines specify eligibility criteria for projects, reporting requirements, and independent verification mechanisms to ensure the 'green' credentials of the bonds.
• It is expected to provide a new avenue for municipal bodies to raise capital for climate-friendly urban development and reduce reliance on traditional funding sources.
• This initiative aligns with India's broader climate finance goals and its commitment to achieving net-zero emissions.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'National Digital Public Infrastructure 2.0' recently in news, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to integrate digital services across all government departments.
2. It primarily focuses on promoting proprietary software solutions for data management.
3. Digital literacy programs are a key component, especially for rural areas.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the initiative aims for seamless integration of digital services. Statement 2 is incorrect; the initiative emphasizes open-source technologies and data sovereignty, not proprietary solutions. Statement 3 is correct, as expansion of digital literacy programs to rural areas is a key pillar.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for setting the benchmark policy interest rates, such as the repo rate, in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: While the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) implements monetary policy, the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is the statutory body entrusted with the responsibility of fixing the benchmark policy interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's Earth Observation Satellites (EOS):
1. They are primarily used for military surveillance only.
2. EOS-07, a recently launched satellite, features high-resolution optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) payloads.
3. Data from EOS satellites is crucial for urban planning, agricultural assessment, and climate change studies.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; Earth Observation Satellites have diverse applications including disaster management, agriculture, urban planning, and climate studies, not just military surveillance. Statement 2 is correct, as EOS-07 is equipped with high-resolution optical and SAR payloads. Statement 3 is correct, as these satellites provide critical data for various civilian applications.

prelimsMocks.question:
With respect to the 'Virasat Darshan' scheme, consider the following statements:
1. It is an initiative by the Ministry of Culture to revive ancient Indian textile traditions.
2. It focuses on developing integrated tourism circuits around lesser-known heritage sites.
3. Community participation and skill development for local guides are key components.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. 'Bharat Vastra Parampara' is the initiative by the Ministry of Culture for textiles. 'Virasat Darshan' is by the Ministry of Tourism. Statements 2 and 3 are correct as the scheme focuses on developing tourism circuits around heritage sites and involves community participation and skill development.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic typically associated with Millets?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Millets are known for their high nutritional value, climate resilience (ability to grow in harsh conditions), and low water footprint, making them sustainable crops. They generally require fewer chemical fertilizers compared to other major cereals, making statement (d) incorrect.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches 'Virasat Darshan' Scheme to Boost Heritage Tourism
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism introduced the 'Virasat Darshan' scheme, a new initiative aimed at promoting India's rich cultural and historical heritage through sustainable tourism.
• The scheme focuses on developing integrated tourism circuits around lesser-known heritage sites, including ancient temples, archaeological ruins, and colonial-era structures.
• It emphasizes community participation, skill development for local guides, and digital promotion to attract both domestic and international tourists.
• Infrastructure development, including last-mile connectivity and visitor amenities, will be a key component of the projects under the scheme.
• The initiative seeks to generate local employment, preserve cultural assets, and enhance the overall tourist experience.

GS1.explainer:

• The plan integrates advanced meteorological forecasting, improved drainage infrastructure, and nature-based solutions like urban wetlands and permeable pavements.
• Focus areas include vulnerability mapping, early warning systems, and capacity building for urban local bodies in disaster preparedness and response.
• Pilot projects will be implemented in ten major flood-prone cities, with a phased rollout across other urban centers.
• The initiative emphasizes inter-agency coordination and citizen participation in developing resilient urban ecosystems.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Fiscal Federalism Dispute
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment upholding the constitutional rights of states in matters of fiscal federalism, resolving a long-standing dispute over revenue sharing.
• The ruling clarified the scope of states' legislative powers regarding certain taxation matters and the distribution of centrally collected revenues.
• The judgment emphasizes cooperative federalism and the need for greater consultation between the Union and State governments on financial policies.
• Experts believe this decision will have significant implications for future fiscal relations and the autonomy of state governments.
• The Court also called for a review mechanism to address imbalances in financial transfers and grants-in-aid.

GS2.headline: National Health Mission Expands Focus to Non-Communicable Diseases in Rural Areas
GS2.explainer:

• The revamped strategy includes enhanced screening facilities at Health and Wellness Centres (HWCs), early detection campaigns, and improved access to treatment for NCDs like diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular diseases.
• Special emphasis will be placed on training community health workers (ASHAs and ANMs) to conduct basic NCD screenings and promote healthy lifestyles.
• The initiative seeks to integrate NCD management with existing primary healthcare services, reducing the need for rural populations to travel to urban centers for diagnosis and care.
• Increased budgetary allocation and public-private partnerships are envisioned to ensure the success and sustainability of the program.

GS2.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Constructive Dialogue on Indus Water Treaty Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• Technical experts from India and Pakistan held a constructive round of talks in Islamabad regarding the implementation of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
• The discussions focused on specific technical issues related to the design and operation of hydroelectric projects on the Western Rivers, as per the treaty provisions.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the IWT, a long-standing framework for water sharing between the two nations.
• While no major breakthroughs were announced, the dialogue was seen as a positive step towards resolving differences through bilateral mechanisms.
• The Permanent Indus Commission is expected to meet again in the coming months to continue these technical deliberations.

GS2.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system and the demarcation of rivers as per the Indus Waters Treaty.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty was signed in 1960 and brokered by the World Bank.

GS2.headline: NITI Aayog Initiates Comprehensive Impact Assessment of Gender Budgeting in India
GS2.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has launched a nationwide impact assessment study to evaluate the effectiveness and outcomes of gender budgeting across various ministries and states.
• The study aims to identify best practices, gaps in implementation, and suggest policy refinements to ensure equitable resource allocation for women's empowerment.
• Data will be collected on the expenditure and impact of schemes designed for women's welfare, education, health, and economic participation.
• This assessment is crucial for strengthening the gender budgeting framework and promoting greater accountability in government spending.
• The findings are expected to inform the upcoming Union Budget and state budgets, advocating for more targeted and impactful gender-sensitive policies.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Tribal Affairs Initiates Review of Forest Rights Act Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs announced a comprehensive review of the implementation of the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006, across various states.
• The review aims to assess the progress in recognizing and vesting forest rights, identify bottlenecks, and ensure the effective empowerment of tribal and forest-dwelling communities.
• Focus areas include the issuance of individual and community forest rights titles, resolution of disputes, and the role of Gram Sabhas in the decision-making process.
• Concerns regarding delays in processing claims, rejection rates, and lack of awareness among beneficiaries will be specifically addressed.
• The findings will inform policy amendments and strengthen the institutional mechanisms for better implementation of the FRA.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor announced a decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% during the latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting.
• This decision comes as retail inflation shows signs of moderation, though global uncertainties persist.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is adopting a cautious 'wait and watch' approach, monitoring food price volatility and global crude oil prices.
• Forecasts for India's GDP growth rate remain robust, prompting the RBI to prioritize price stability.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Gen Earth Observation Satellite for Disaster Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-07) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
• EOS-07 is equipped with high-resolution optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) payloads, designed for enhanced monitoring of natural disasters.
• The satellite will provide critical data for urban planning, agricultural assessment, and climate change studies, with improved temporal and spatial resolution.
• This mission marks a significant step towards India's self-reliance in space technology and its commitment to global disaster risk reduction efforts.
• The data from EOS-07 is expected to bolster India's capabilities in providing early warnings for floods, cyclones, and droughts.

GS3.headline: India Set to Exceed 2030 Renewable Energy Targets, Eyes Further Ambitions
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced that India is well on track to surpass its 2030 target of 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity.
• Rapid deployment of solar and wind energy projects, coupled with advancements in battery storage technology, is driving this accelerated progress.
• The government is now exploring even more ambitious targets for renewable energy, potentially aiming for net-zero emissions earlier than initially planned.
• Green hydrogen initiatives and offshore wind projects are identified as key growth areas for the next decade.
• This achievement underscores India's commitment to climate action and energy security, attracting significant foreign investment in the sector.

GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest producer of renewable energy in the world, after China and the USA.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Draft 'AI Ethics and Governance Framework' for Public Consultation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a comprehensive draft framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible development and deployment of AI technologies, focusing on principles of fairness, accountability, transparency, and data privacy.
• It proposes guidelines for industries, researchers, and government agencies on ethical AI practices, including bias mitigation and human oversight.
• Public consultation is invited to gather feedback from stakeholders, including tech companies, civil society organizations, and academic institutions.
• This move positions India as a proactive player in shaping global AI governance standards, balancing innovation with societal well-being.

GS3.headline: National Cyber Security Coordinator Urges Robust Framework for Critical Infrastructure Protection
GS3.explainer:
• The National Cyber Security Coordinator emphasized the urgent need for a robust and dynamic cybersecurity framework to protect India's critical information infrastructure (CII).
• Addressing a conference on cyber resilience, he highlighted the escalating threat of state-sponsored cyber-attacks and ransomware campaigns targeting essential services.
• The call includes enhanced collaboration between government agencies, private sector entities, and international partners to share threat intelligence and best practices.
• Focus areas identified are capacity building, development of indigenous cybersecurity solutions, and stringent audit mechanisms for CII.
• The government plans to introduce new regulations to mandate stricter cybersecurity standards for sectors like energy, finance, and telecommunications.

GS3.headline: Government Launches New Scheme to Promote Millet Cultivation and Consumption
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a new national scheme aimed at significantly boosting the cultivation, processing, and consumption of millets.
• The initiative, 'Poshan Anaj Abhiyan', offers incentives to farmers for adopting millet farming, provides support for establishing processing units, and promotes millet-based products.
• It aligns with the International Year of Millets (2023) objectives and India's commitment to food and nutritional security.
• Millets are recognized for their climate resilience, nutritional value, and low water footprint, making them a sustainable crop choice.
• The scheme also includes awareness campaigns to educate consumers about the health benefits of incorporating millets into their diet.

GS3.headline: India Plans Modernization of Antarctic Research Stations for Enhanced Scientific Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Earth Sciences announced plans for a significant upgrade and modernization of India's permanent research stations in Antarctica, Maitri and Bharati.
• The modernization aims to enhance scientific capabilities, improve logistical support, and ensure the safety and comfort of researchers in extreme Antarctic conditions.
• New state-of-the-art laboratories, advanced communication systems, and sustainable energy solutions are part of the upgrade.
• This initiative underscores India's commitment to Antarctic research, climate change studies, and adherence to the Antarctic Treaty System.
• The upgraded facilities will support long-term studies in glaciology, oceanography, atmospheric sciences, and polar biology.

GS3.headline: New Subsidy Program to Boost Smart Farming Technology Adoption Among Small Farmers
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced a new subsidy scheme to encourage small and marginal farmers to adopt smart farming technologies.
• The program provides financial assistance for purchasing drones for crop monitoring, IoT-enabled sensors for soil health, and precision irrigation systems.
• It aims to enhance agricultural productivity, reduce input costs, and improve climate resilience for vulnerable farming communities.
• Training and technical support will be provided through Krishi Vigyan Kendras (KVKs) and agricultural universities.
• This initiative is part of a broader push to modernize Indian agriculture and leverage technology for sustainable food production.

GS3.headline: Researchers Discover New Species in Western Ghats, Highlighting Biodiversity Hotspot Status
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian researchers announced the discovery of several new species of amphibians and invertebrates in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats.
• The findings underscore the critical importance of the Western Ghats as a global biodiversity hotspot and the need for intensified conservation efforts.
• The newly identified species include a unique frog variant and several previously unrecorded insect species, contributing to the region's ecological diversity.
• Scientists emphasize the threats posed by habitat loss, climate change, and pollution to the fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats.
• The discoveries call for stronger protective measures and sustainable development practices to safeguard this natural treasure.

GS3.headline: SEBI Issues Guidelines for Municipal Corporations to Raise Funds through Green Bonds
GS3.explainer:
• The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) released detailed guidelines enabling municipal corporations to issue 'Green Bonds' to finance environmentally sustainable projects.
• This move aims to channel private capital towards urban green infrastructure, renewable energy projects, and sustainable waste management initiatives at the local level.
• The guidelines specify eligibility criteria for projects, reporting requirements, and independent verification mechanisms to ensure the 'green' credentials of the bonds.
• It is expected to provide a new avenue for municipal bodies to raise capital for climate-friendly urban development and reduce reliance on traditional funding sources.
• This initiative aligns with India's broader climate finance goals and its commitment to achieving net-zero emissions.
